column2: 1. The course provided an opportunity to develop relevant learning and competencies.
column3: 2. Textbooks/reading materials used in the course were appropriate.
column4: 3. Total time allocated for the course is appropriate to cover all the contents.
column5: 4. The contents specified in the syllabus for the course were covered.

column6: The course was very informative and well organized. It provided a clear understanding of the key concepts and connected theory with practical examples. Overall, it was a very engaging and valuable learning experience.
Consider more time for the course. Keep attention  during whole 3 hours is not appropriate. 2 hours per day will be better. Day can increase rather than hour.

Since this is the first batch, we recognize scheduling difficulties, from next batch onward and academic calendar, preferably for the whole year would be appreciated. It will help students especially who are professionals to plan for work, and attend class accordingly. Thanks.

The course provided a strong basic foundation, and all the facilitators were excellent. I found the materials very useful and relevant. During the upcoming Eid vacation, I am planning to read a few of the books that were recommended in the course materials.

I would like to make a few suggestions for future courses:

Kamal Uddin Sir’s class would have been even more impactful if it were conducted offline. If external guest facilitators are in the country, it would be great if their sessions could be arranged in person.

The online presentation felt quite stressful. I would like to request that presentations be arranged offline in future courses if possible.

It would be helpful to include more online interactive games or activities to maintain participants’ focus and attention during virtual sessions.

Thank you to all the facilitators and organizers for such a meaningful course.

The course MHS 5101 was highly informative and well-structured. It provided a clear understanding of key mental health concepts. The course materials, time allocation, and syllabus coverage were very appropriate and supportive for effective learning.

This course was very effective for MHPSS, and the information that was provided was also useful and relevant to me. Overall, I am satisfied to be a part of the course and also the class presentation; time management was excellent.
It's was effective course and designed so relatively.
Need more in person classes. All online is killing my productivity! I mean it!

The overall course was quite well structured and the reading materials were enlightening. However, the overall time allocated for the course was quite tight and it was difficult to finish all the assignments and readings within time. For such good content, a little extra time would really help to gather more knowledge on the topics.

As the cource is design for professionals, so it is recommendaed to reduce the long assignment writing, the tools of student evaluations tools can use differently. so that the professionals can get chance to manage their work life balance.

I don't encourage students to say random sentences using relevant keywords to earn credits in my class. I can't do that as a student, either. If it is considered standard for performance at BracU, whether during classroom discussion or Q&A, I'm a bit confused about grading policies. And I respectfully refuse to fit into such a standard, I don't believe. 

Regarding group presentation, all I can say is I saved my group when they left me with 2 minutes to finish 2 questions. If I'm not wrong, as per the prior instructions, the questions were supposed to be specific to the group members who prepared that part. I waited for questions relevant to my part. As I strongly believe, i) everyone deserves full credit for their part, whether it is preparing the slides, presenting them, or answering the questions, and ii) group work isn't meant to compete with each other for the limelight, but rather to compete together. Only because I didn't interfere with my group members' Q&A, and some of my members said something during my Q&A that didn't specifically answer those questions (which is justifiable, as they didn't prepare those parts), I'm being discredited. Moreover, the groups with zero feedback and those with plenty during the session received the same score, made me more confused about the grading strategies.
